pr.title: Fixup ftmaterial function call, cleanup itdrop
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Small merged cleanup/fix PR touching fighter material initialization and item drop code. In ftmaterial.c, ftMaterial_800BF260 now passes hsdMObj.parent as the parent class argument to hsdInitClassInfo instead of ftMObj.parent, correcting the class hierarchy setup for ft_mobj. In itdrop.c, direct item_gobj->user_data casts/accesses were replaced with the GET_ITEM macro after including inlines.h, making item access consistent with project conventions.

what_changed.change: Changed ftMaterial_800BF260 so hsdInitClassInfo initializes ftMObj.parent with &hsdMObj.parent as the base/parent class parameter rather than &ftMObj.parent.
what_changed.why_it_matters: This better represents ft_mobj inheriting from or being based on the existing HSD_MObj class metadata instead of self-referencing ftMObj.parent during class registration.
Needed to use the GET_ITEM helper macro.

what_changed.change: Replaced three direct item GObj user_data accesses with GET_ITEM(item_gobj) or GET_ITEM(spawned_item_gobj).
what_changed.why_it_matters: Centralizes item payload extraction behind the established inline/macro convention and avoids open-coded user_data access in item code.

smart_moves: The ftmaterial fix is minimal but semantically important: only the parent class argument to hsdInitClassInfo changed, preserving the rest of the function and likely minimizing matching risk.
The itdrop cleanup uses the existing GET_ITEM abstraction rather than introducing new casts or helper names.
Adding inlines.h locally in itdrop.c keeps the macro dependency explicit at the file using GET_ITEM.
decomp_lessons: When registering HSD class info, verify that the parent/base class pointer is the existing parent class metadata, not the struct being initialized. Self-referential class initialization can be a subtle decomp or cleanup error.
Prefer project-provided access macros such as GET_ITEM over direct gobj->user_data access when retrieving typed item data.
Small semantic fixes can be isolated to a single argument change to avoid disturbing surrounding matched code.
naming_conventions: Fighter material functions in this area retain generated-address names such as ftMaterial_800BF260 and ftMaterial_800BF2B8.
Item drop functions retain generated-address names such as it_8026F3D4, it_8026F6BC, and it_8026F7C8.
Typed item object variables use names like item, spawned_item, item_gobj, and spawned_item_gobj.
The item accessor macro is named GET_ITEM and expects an item GObj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: For class initialization calls, matching may depend on passing the correct global class info symbol as an argument; here &hsdMObj.parent replaced &ftMObj.parent.
Macro replacement from gobj->user_data to GET_ITEM should be checked for codegen equivalence, but the diff suggests this was intended as a cleanup rather than a behavior rewrite.
No assembly diff, permuter notes, or reviewer matching discussion were present in the PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other direct item_gobj->user_data accesses that should use GET_ITEM.
Audit other hsdInitClassInfo calls for accidental self-parent arguments.
Check whether other fighter-specific HSD object info structs use hsdMObj.parent or equivalent parent metadata consistently.
